items.briefing:
The Australian Cyber Security Centre has warned that credentials for Fortinet firewalls and VPN gateways have been exposed, meaning attackers may already have login details for these devices used in many Australian workplaces. If your organisation uses Fortinet equipment, IT teams should check for unauthorised access and update passwords immediately. Separately, a Cloudflare report highlights rising cyberattacks targeting civil society organisations, with Australian groups among those affected — charities, advocacy bodies, and community organisations should not assume they are too small to be targeted.

**Practical tip:** If your workplace uses a VPN to connect remotely, ask your IT team whether it runs Fortinet software and confirm it has been checked for unauthorised access.

featured.title: ACSC warns of reported credential exposure affecting Fortinet firewalls and VPN gateways
featured.quote: ACSC warns of widespread credential exposure affecting critical Fortinet firewalls and VPN gateways used by Australian organisations.
